subTopicTitle: Waiting for Godot (Beckett) — Act II — Memory/forgetting

items.prompt: Define the role of memory and forgetting in Act II of 'Waiting for Godot'.
keyPoints: Memory plays a significant role in character interactions
Forgetting exemplifies the cyclic nature of the play
The characters' selective memory highlights existential themes

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: List at least three instances of memory or forgetting in Act II.
keyPoints: Estragon forgets the events of the previous day
Vladimir recalls happier times but questions their reality
Pozzo forgets having met Estragon and Vladimir before

markingCriteria.full: Includes all instances with brief descriptions

items.prompt: Explain how Beckett uses repetition and forgetfulness as a theme in Act II.
keyPoints: Repetition emphasizes the monotony of existence
Forgetting adds to the absurdity and confusion
Both elements underscore the futility and cyclic nature of life

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Vladimir tries to remind Estragon of the events from the previous day. How does this reflect the theme of unreliable memory?
keyPoints: Demonstrates the fragility of human memory
Shows contrast between characters' perceptions of reality
Highlights existential themes of uncertainty and doubt

The absurdity of human experiences being forgotten
